infobox.title: Linux Setup — Omarchy
infobox.imageCaption: Hyprland Wayland tiling WM with Alacritty terminal

lead:
The Linux setup runs **Arch Linux via Omarchy** — an opinionated 'omakase' developer distribution created by DHH (David Heinemeier Hansson, creator of Ruby on Rails). Omarchy ships with Hyprland Wayland tiling WM, Neovim, Alacritty, Ghostty, and Tmux — a curated, pre-configured developer environment that skips weeks of manual configuration.

The username is `libre` — consistent with the FOSS-first philosophy evident across the [[privacy-stack|Privacy Stack]] and [[foss-coin|FOSS Coin]] project. Omarchy was forked to a personal GitHub repository to allow selective control over which upstream updates get applied.

content.content:
**Omarchy** is described by its creator as 'omakase Arch Linux' — *omakase* being the Japanese restaurant concept where the chef selects the best dishes on your behalf. DHH applied this to Arch Linux: instead of spending weeks configuring a desktop from scratch, Omarchy gives you a complete, opinionated setup in 15–30 minutes.

The repository has ~22,000 GitHub stars (as of April 2026). It is installed from an ISO, wipes the dedicated drive, and applies full-disk encryption by default. The Super key is the command centre for all shortcuts.
The daily workflow is keyboard-driven and terminal-centric. Almost no GUI applications are used for development work.

rows.Role: Session and window management — persistent sessions survive terminal restarts
Primary editor — learning Lua for config customisation
Fuzzy file find → open in editor
AI agent in accept-all mode — terminal-native pair programmer
On-demand socket activation — reduces idle RAM on constrained homelab

content.title: Omarchy v3.4+ changes
content.content: Version 3.4 added Tmux by default (t alias), Claude Code as the default AI agent (cx alias, accept-all mode), hibernation enabled by default, and AI agent Tmux layouts: tdl, tdlm, tsl.

The daily workflow integrates **Claude Code** (called 'P.I.' — Personal Intelligence — in this setup) as a terminal-native AI agent. It is configured with a custom `CLAUDE.md` at `/home/libre/CLAUDE.md` that describes the personal workflow context.

Claude Code in accept-all mode (`cx` alias) runs autonomously — it can read files, write code, and execute shell commands without requiring confirmation for each step. The Omarchy skill file (`github.com/robzolkos/omarchy-skill`) extends this with Omarchy-specific context.

This integration represents a shift in how terminal work is done: rather than memorising command syntax, the agent is queried in natural language and executes the appropriate commands.

OBS Studio is used for screen recording and video production, with a specific audio configuration:
- **Noise Gate:** Close threshold −32 dB / Open threshold −26 dB — eliminates background hiss between speech
- **RNNoise suppression:** AI-based noise cancellation plugin
- **Mic:** CMF Buds 2 Pro via PulseAudio

**Shotcut** is the video editor. The entire production pipeline is Linux-native and free software — no Adobe products.

content.title: Accidental terminal removal
content.content: Once accidentally removed all terminal emulators while cleaning packages. Recovery path: Ctrl+Alt+F2 to drop to TTY2, log in, reinstall Alacritty or Ghostty via pacman from the framebuffer terminal. The TTY is always available even when the graphical session has no terminals.

editHistory.summary: Full write: terminal workflow, AI integration, media setup
